stories.title: A nationwide rain band becomes the dominant Australian weather story
stories.summary: A broad north-west cloudband is forecast to spread rain across every state and territory, making it one of the more consequential May systems in years. For farmers and drought-hit districts, the upside is moisture relief; for transport and local communities, the main risk is flooding, road disruption and patchy forecast uncertainty around where the heaviest falls land.

links.headline: First north-west cloudband of 2026 to soak all states
links.excerpt: Public broadcaster coverage frames the system as an unusually widespread May rain event with both drought relief and disruption risks.
links.imageAlt: First north-west cloudband of 2026 to soak all states

links.headline: Weather - Australia 7 Day Forecasts & Weather Radar
links.excerpt: Specialist forecast coverage adds maps, radar and short-range guidance that are useful for following where the rain band is actually tracking.

links.headline: Australian rainfall and weather outlook
links.excerpt: The BOM page is the core official source for rainfall projections, warning products and forecast updates as the cloudband moves east.

stories.imageAlt: First north-west cloudband of 2026 to soak all states

stories.title: Canberra funds a stronger passenger-rights regime for airlines
stories.summary: The federal budget has put money behind a new aviation consumer protection framework, including an ombuds-style complaints path and stronger oversight of airline disruption handling. The political question now is whether the eventual scheme looks more like European compensation rules or a lighter-touch Australian model shaped around competition and route viability.

links.headline: Federal budget allocates $40m to fund an overhaul of airline passenger rights
links.excerpt: ABC focuses on the new consumer-protection funding and the debate over whether compensation rights should become automatic.
links.imageAlt: Federal budget allocates $40m to fund an overhaul of airline passenger rights

links.headline: Budget flags $40m+ for airline competition and consumer protection
links.excerpt: Trade coverage adds sector detail on the ombuds scheme, ACCC monitoring and how airlines may respond to tougher rules.
links.imageAlt: Budget flags $40m+ for airline competition and consumer protection

links.headline: Aviation consumer ombuds scheme: progress so far and next steps
links.excerpt: The department page is the primary policy source on the ombuds design, consultation track and implementation steps.

stories.imageAlt: Federal budget allocates $40m to fund an overhaul of airline passenger rights

stories.title: Australia's diphtheria outbreak is now a national public-health warning
stories.summary: The first reported Australian diphtheria death in nearly a decade has pushed what had been a remote outbreak into broader national attention. Health authorities are stressing vaccination, rapid diagnosis and the heightened risk in remote Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities where respiratory and skin infections can spread more easily.

links.headline: Diphtheria used to be a major cause of childhood deaths. Now it's back in Australia
links.excerpt: ABC gives the clearest general explainer on how the outbreak has grown and why lower immunity and access gaps matter.
links.imageAlt: Diphtheria used to be a major cause of childhood deaths. Now it's back in Australia

links.headline: Diphtheria in Australia - Epidemiological update - 11 May 2026
links.excerpt: The national epidemiology update is the best primary-source snapshot of cases, locations, testing and clinical context.

links.headline: Diphtheria alert for regional Western Australia
links.excerpt: WA's health alert adds the strongest local guidance on symptoms, treatment and the Kimberley-centered outbreak picture.

stories.imageAlt: Diphtheria used to be a major cause of childhood deaths. Now it's back in Australia

stories.title: A Chinese undersea sensor near Lombok sharpens Australia's submarine debate
stories.summary: The device found near the Lombok Strait is being read in Australia less as an isolated curiosity and more as evidence of a wider Chinese effort to map chokepoints and undersea conditions. That matters directly to AUKUS, regional naval movements and the broader argument that Australia needs more resilient undersea sensing and autonomous systems, not just crewed submarines.

links.headline: What the undersea monitoring device found in Indonesia tells us about China's maritime strategy
links.excerpt: The analysis piece connects the discovery to anti-submarine warfare, chokepoint monitoring and wider Chinese maritime strategy.
links.imageAlt: What the undersea monitoring device found in Indonesia tells us about China's maritime strategy

links.headline: Device found near Bali and Lombok identified as Chinese undersea monitoring system
links.excerpt: ABC's original report covers the identification of the device itself and why analysts view the waterway as strategically important.
links.imageAlt: Device found near Bali and Lombok identified as Chinese undersea monitoring system

links.headline: China is mapping the ocean floor as it prepares for submarine warfare with the US
links.excerpt: The Reuters mirror provides broader international framing on seabed mapping and military undersea competition.

stories.imageAlt: What the undersea monitoring device found in Indonesia tells us about China's maritime strategy

stories.title: Migration, housing and bracket-creep politics are merging into one budget fight
stories.summary: The opposition is tying immigration more tightly to housing construction while also pushing tax-bracket indexation and restrictions on benefits for non-citizens. The policy package is designed to hit a cost-of-living nerve, but its weak points are implementation detail, long-run budget cost and the effect on universities, employers and visa holders.

links.headline: Coalition proposes plan to end tax bracket creep
links.excerpt: ABC outlines the opposition's tax-indexation pitch and its political attempt to reset the post-budget debate.
links.imageAlt: Coalition proposes plan to end tax bracket creep

links.headline: Angus Taylor accused of dog-whistling and marginalising immigrants over plan to cut benefits to non-citizens
links.excerpt: Guardian coverage focuses on the social and political backlash to the migration-and-benefits part of the package.
links.imageAlt: Angus Taylor accused of dog-whistling and marginalising immigrants over plan to cut benefits to non-citizens

links.headline: Federal budget 2026: How migration is being reshaped
links.excerpt: SBS adds practical detail on the migration-program changes already in the budget and what they mean for skilled entrants.
links.imageAlt: Federal budget 2026: How migration is being reshaped

stories.imageAlt: Coalition proposes plan to end tax bracket creep

stories.title: Australia expands its crackdown on organised neo-Nazi activity
stories.summary: Canberra has now formally listed White Australia, previously linked to the National Socialist Network, as a prohibited hate group. The immediate effect is legal: support, funding, recruitment and organising become criminal risks; the next test is whether the network simply rebrands again and challenges the law in court.

links.headline: Australia bans a neo-Nazi network under new law that criminalizes hate groups
links.excerpt: AP gives the cleanest international summary of the ban and the new penalties attached to backing listed groups.
links.imageAlt: Australia bans a neo-Nazi network under new law that criminalizes hate groups

links.headline: Neo-Nazi group banned in Australia under hate laws
links.excerpt: ABC explains how the listing works under the new federal framework and why ministers say phoenix rebranding will not avoid it.
links.imageAlt: Neo-Nazi group banned in Australia under hate laws

links.headline: Neo-Nazis fundraising to challenge laws that listed them as hate group
links.excerpt: The follow-up report adds reporting on legal challenge planning, overseas extremist ties and movement resilience.
links.imageAlt: Neo-Nazis fundraising to challenge laws that listed them as hate group

stories.imageAlt: Australia bans a neo-Nazi network under new law that criminalizes hate groups

stories.title: Kyiv is still counting the dead after one of the heaviest Russian attack waves in months
stories.summary: Russia's recent multi-day drone and missile barrages have undercut the latest talk of de-escalation and left Kyiv mourning one of the capital's deadliest strikes of the year. The pattern looks familiar: diplomatic rhetoric rises, then battlefield and civilian pressure immediately returns, reinforcing Ukraine's case that Russia is still trying to grind down cities and air defences rather than move seriously toward peace.

links.headline: Russia hammers Ukraine for a 3rd straight day, flattening a Kyiv apartment block and killing 9
links.excerpt: AP's earlier dispatch captures the scale and timing of the barrage as it unfolded across multiple regions.
links.imageAlt: Russia hammers Ukraine for a 3rd straight day, flattening a Kyiv apartment block and killing 9

links.headline: Kyiv mourns as death toll from Russian attack in the Ukrainian capital rises to 24
links.excerpt: This follow-up moves from immediate damage to casualty updates, mourning and the strike's political resonance.
links.imageAlt: Kyiv mourns as death toll from Russian attack in the Ukrainian capital rises to 24

links.headline: Ukraine war briefing: Zelenskyy vows retribution over deadly Russian bombardments
links.excerpt: Guardian's briefing rounds up the civilian toll, Russian energy-target strikes and the broader diplomatic backdrop.
links.imageAlt: Ukraine war briefing: Zelenskyy vows retribution over deadly Russian bombardments

stories.imageAlt: Russia hammers Ukraine for a 3rd straight day, flattening a Kyiv apartment block and killing 9

stories.title: The peace track looks thinner as China, Congress and drone warfare crowd the frame
stories.summary: Talk of a negotiated end is being overtaken by the old fundamentals: Russian attack volume, Western military support and major-power positioning around Moscow. Putin's planned trip to Beijing and a House push for more Ukraine support and Russia sanctions both suggest the war's next phase is still being shaped by coalition politics and industrial endurance, not by any near-term settlement.

links.headline: Putin to visit Chinese leader Xi Jinping days after Trump's trip to Beijing
links.excerpt: AP highlights the symbolic and practical importance of Beijing ties as Russia remains reliant on China economically and diplomatically.
links.imageAlt: Putin to visit Chinese leader Xi Jinping days after Trump's trip to Beijing

links.headline: Supporters of bill to aid Ukraine and sanction Russia hit number to force House vote
links.excerpt: This piece focuses on the U.S. domestic machinery that could still shape the scale and durability of support for Kyiv.
links.imageAlt: Supporters of bill to aid Ukraine and sanction Russia hit number to force House vote

links.headline: Six dead as Russia launches over 800 drones at Ukraine
links.excerpt: ABC adds the military and air-defence angle, showing how attack volume itself has become part of Russia's strategy.
links.imageAlt: Six dead as Russia launches over 800 drones at Ukraine

stories.imageAlt: Putin to visit Chinese leader Xi Jinping days after Trump's trip to Beijing

stories.title: Trump leaves Beijing without the Iran breakthrough he wanted
stories.summary: Trump's China trip produced atmospherics, big claims and some rhetorical alignment about ending the Iran war, but little concrete leverage over Tehran or a clear reopening path for Hormuz. The broader takeaway is that Washington still wants Beijing's help, yet Beijing remains incentivised to avoid owning Trump's war or giving him an easy diplomatic win.

links.headline: US President Donald Trump leaves Beijing with no major breakthroughs on Iran war
links.excerpt: ABC's write-up keeps the focus on what Trump did not actually secure from Xi despite the summit theatre.
links.imageAlt: US President Donald Trump leaves Beijing with no major breakthroughs on Iran war

links.headline: Trump downplays differences with China's Xi over Iran as he heads to Beijing for high-stakes summit
links.excerpt: AP provides the clearest pre-summit framing on why Iran, trade and Taiwan were colliding in the same visit.
links.imageAlt: Trump downplays differences with China's Xi over Iran as he heads to Beijing for high-stakes summit

links.headline: Trump leaves China without breakthroughs on Iran, Taiwan or AI
links.excerpt: Guardian emphasizes the lack of substantive outcomes and the strategic gains Beijing may have made from simply hosting the summit.
links.imageAlt: Trump leaves China without breakthroughs on Iran, Taiwan or AI

stories.imageAlt: US President Donald Trump leaves Beijing with no major breakthroughs on Iran war

stories.title: Iran diplomacy remains brittle even after the latest ceasefire push
stories.summary: The working problem is trust: Iran says Washington's demands and rhetoric still do not look credible, while Trump has publicly dismissed Tehran's latest proposal and warned the ceasefire is close to collapsing. That leaves mediation alive but fragile, with both sides talking about an end to the war while still shaping terms through public pressure and strategic ambiguity.

links.headline: Iran's top diplomat says a lack of trust is impeding talks to end war with the US
links.excerpt: AP centers the Iranian position that distrust, not just technical differences, is the main barrier to an agreement.
links.imageAlt: Iran's top diplomat says a lack of trust is impeding talks to end war with the US

links.headline: Iran responds to US ceasefire proposal but Trump rejects it as unacceptable
links.excerpt: This AP report captures the hardening U.S. public line after Tehran sent back its latest formal response.
links.imageAlt: Iran responds to US ceasefire proposal but Trump rejects it as unacceptable

links.headline: Trump says ceasefire with Iran on life support after rejecting peace proposals
links.excerpt: Guardian adds the White House political framing and the risk that Hormuz security again becomes a bargaining chip.
links.imageAlt: Trump says ceasefire with Iran on life support after rejecting peace proposals

stories.imageAlt: Iran's top diplomat says a lack of trust is impeding talks to end war with the US

stories.title: Israel says it killed Hamas's military wing leader in Gaza
stories.summary: Israel says an airstrike killed Izz al-Din al-Haddad, one of the last surviving senior Hamas military commanders tied to the October 7 attacks. Even if confirmed, the tactical success does not simplify the wider Gaza picture: Palestinian casualties continue, humanitarian conditions remain dire and the narrative split between military-target and civilian-cost reporting is still sharp.

links.headline: Israeli strike kills Hamas' military wing leader, who Israel says was an architect of Oct. 7 attacks
links.excerpt: AP gives the strongest single-source update on the Israeli claim, the target's status and what remains unclear.
links.imageAlt: Israeli strike kills Hamas' military wing leader, who Israel says was an architect of Oct. 7 attacks

links.headline: Israel targets Hamas leader, kills seven Palestinians
links.excerpt: ABC keeps both the Israeli military claim and the reported Palestinian civilian deaths in the same frame.
links.imageAlt: Israel targets Hamas leader, kills seven Palestinians

links.headline: Seven killed in Gaza on Nakba Day as Israel says it targets Hamas member
links.excerpt: Al Jazeera places stronger emphasis on civilian casualties and Palestinian humanitarian framing around the strike.
links.imageAlt: Seven killed in Gaza on Nakba Day as Israel says it targets Hamas member

stories.imageAlt: Israeli strike kills Hamas' military wing leader, who Israel says was an architect of Oct. 7 attacks

stories.title: Back in Washington, Trump faces inflation that his summit optics did not fix
stories.summary: Trump returned from Beijing to a domestic economy where inflation has re-accelerated, especially through energy. The political danger is straightforward: if oil, transport and consumer prices stay elevated while the administration is seen as overpromising on Iran and trade, foreign-policy spectacle turns into a cost-of-living liability at home.

links.headline: Brass bands in Beijing make way for sticker shock at home as Trump returns to escalating inflation
links.excerpt: AP ties the summit comedown directly to consumer-price pressure, wage erosion and Trump-era tariff and war effects.
links.imageAlt: Brass bands in Beijing make way for sticker shock at home as Trump returns to escalating inflation

links.headline: Consumer Price Index News Release - 2026 M04 Results
links.excerpt: The April CPI release is the primary-source data behind the renewed inflation story, with energy doing much of the monthly lifting.

links.headline: Brass bands in Beijing make way for sticker shock at home as Trump returns to escalating inflation
links.excerpt: ABC's wire mirror is useful if the AP page is busy or cached poorly, and carries the same core reporting.
links.imageAlt: Brass bands in Beijing make way for sticker shock at home as Trump returns to escalating inflation

stories.imageAlt: Brass bands in Beijing make way for sticker shock at home as Trump returns to escalating inflation

stories.title: The ICC case against Ronald dela Rosa has turned into a live Philippine institutional crisis
stories.summary: This remains the one Philippines story that clearly belongs in a broad digest: the ICC warrant against Senator Ronald dela Rosa has spilled into a Senate standoff, gunfire chaos and a deeper test of how far Philippine institutions will go to shield Duterte-era figures. The issue now sits at the junction of criminal accountability, elite factional politics and the legitimacy of the state's post-Duterte reckoning.

links.headline: ICC unseals arrest warrant for a prominent Philippine senator over drug war killings under Duterte
links.excerpt: AP's first report explains the legal basis of the warrant and why the ICC is targeting a serving senator.
links.imageAlt: ICC unseals arrest warrant for a prominent Philippine senator over drug war killings under Duterte

links.headline: Gunfire breaks out in Philippine Senate where authorities tried to arrest a senator
links.excerpt: This follow-up captures how the legal case immediately escalated into physical confrontation and institutional disorder.
links.imageAlt: Gunfire breaks out in Philippine Senate where authorities tried to arrest a senator

links.headline: Timeline: Dela Rosa's May 13 standoff, Senate lockdown and gunfire
links.excerpt: Philstar adds the best local chronology of who moved where, when the lockdown began and how dela Rosa got out.
links.imageAlt: Timeline: Dela Rosa's May 13 standoff, Senate lockdown and gunfire

links.headline: Philippine politician wanted by ICC flees Senate
links.excerpt: Al Jazeera gives a rights-and-accountability framing that is useful alongside AP's institutional reporting.
links.imageAlt: Philippine politician wanted by ICC flees Senate

stories.imageAlt: ICC unseals arrest warrant for a prominent Philippine senator over drug war killings under Duterte

stories.title: Tesla's robotaxi filings are now exposing more of the human backup behind autonomy
stories.summary: Newly unredacted crash narratives show remote teleoperators were involved in multiple low-speed Tesla robotaxi incidents. That shifts the debate away from the marketing label of autonomy alone and toward a harder question: whether the human-in-the-loop safety layer is actually reliable, scalable and honestly described.

links.headline: Tesla reveals two robotaxi crashes involving teleoperators
links.excerpt: TechCrunch anchors the story in the NHTSA filings and the disclosure of the teleoperator role.
links.imageAlt: Tesla reveals two robotaxi crashes involving teleoperators

links.headline: Tesla finally reveals what happened in 17 Robotaxi crashes
links.excerpt: Electrek adds deeper EV-specialist reading of the newly unredacted narratives and where the concerning cases sit.
links.quality: EV specialist outlet with Tesla scrutiny
links.imageAlt: Tesla finally reveals what happened in 17 Robotaxi crashes

links.headline: Tesla Reveals New Details About Robotaxi Crashes - and the Humans Involved
links.excerpt: WIRED emphasizes the human-factors and safety-governance angle rather than just the crash count.
links.quality: Tech magazine, safety and regulatory emphasis
links.imageAlt: Tesla Reveals New Details About Robotaxi Crashes - and the Humans Involved

stories.imageAlt: Tesla reveals two robotaxi crashes involving teleoperators

stories.title: OpenAI is testing whether ChatGPT can become a personal-finance dashboard
stories.summary: OpenAI's new personal-finance preview for U.S. Pro users pushes ChatGPT closer to a high-trust consumer workflow by linking bank and investment data through Plaid. The product logic is obvious, but so are the risks: financial hallucinations, privacy expectations and the burden of presenting uncertain advice inside a conversational UI.

links.headline: Personal finance in ChatGPT
links.excerpt: The company post describes account connections, portfolio views, spending analysis and the intended user experience.

links.headline: OpenAI launches ChatGPT for personal finance, will let you connect bank accounts
links.excerpt: TechCrunch turns the launch into a practical feature overview with an emphasis on connected accounts and dashboards.
links.imageAlt: OpenAI launches ChatGPT for personal finance, will let you connect bank accounts

links.headline: OpenAI rolls out ChatGPT Personal Finance tool with real-time bank account connectivity
links.excerpt: This report adds a startup-media framing on Plaid-backed connectivity and the broader product positioning.
links.imageAlt: OpenAI rolls out ChatGPT Personal Finance tool with real-time bank account connectivity

stories.imageAlt: OpenAI launches ChatGPT for personal finance, will let you connect bank accounts

stories.title: GoPro is another civilian tech brand drifting toward defence money
stories.summary: GoPro's strategic review is being read not just as a corporate sale story but as another sign that struggling consumer-hardware businesses see defence and aerospace as a path to relevance and margin. Even if no deal lands soon, the direction fits a wider pattern of dual-use imaging and drone-adjacent companies looking for tougher, government-backed demand.

links.headline: Even GoPro is pivoting to defense
links.excerpt: TechCrunch is the sharpest short take on why defence applications are now part of the company's strategic logic.
links.imageAlt: Even GoPro is pivoting to defense

links.headline: GoPro founder fully supports action camera giant's potential sale
links.excerpt: Camera-industry coverage adds product-business context and management signalling around a sale or merger path.
links.imageAlt: GoPro founder fully supports action camera giant's potential sale

links.headline: GoPro's future just took a dramatic turn as defense firms and imaging giants circle the company
links.excerpt: T3 brings the strategic-options framing together in a consumer-tech voice that is still useful for market direction.
links.imageAlt: GoPro's future just took a dramatic turn as defense firms and imaging giants circle the company

stories.imageAlt: Even GoPro is pivoting to defense
